title: Outcome Contract, Non-Goals, and Measures of Success · Decision Evidence Lens: Stop Pivot Rule
summary: The proposed product promises engagement and delight but names no user decision, baseline, guardrail, accessibility outcome, or unacceptable harm. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 stop pivot rule increment in measurable outcome and non-goal contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows.
State non-goals, excluded users or cases, unsupported claims, and the evidence required to expand scope.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Stop Pivot Rule · Theory Stop Pivot Rule
steps.instruction:
theory case — A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Stop Pivot Rule contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Stop Pivot Rule · Theory Stop Pivot Rule.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows. For Predict Stop Pivot Rule · Theory Stop Pivot Rule, keep Stop Pivot Rule connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Stop Pivot Rule condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct — Controls public-good reasoning, harm avoidance, honesty, fairness, privacy, security, competence, review, and accountability.

hints: For Predict Stop Pivot Rule · Theory Stop Pivot Rule, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Stop Pivot Rule precondition, or invariant that must hold.

The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows.

For Predict Stop Pivot Rule · Theory Stop Pivot Rule, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Stopping after negative evidence means the project failed.

options.text: Stopping after negative evidence means the project failed. That claim does not survive the predict stop pivot rule evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. Evidence target: Bound Stop Pivot Rule · Theory Stop Pivot Rule.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows. content.text: Define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. The worked example variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows. A defensible solution applies Stop Pivot Rule, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to measurable outcome and non-goal contract.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-2
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Stopping after negative evidence means the project failed. This fails the Stop Pivot Rule contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

stimulus.title: Stop Pivot Rule failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Stopping after negative evidence means the project failed.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Stop Pivot Rule, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the stop pivot rule defect evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the Stop Pivot Rule model · Theory Stop Pivot Rule
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Stop Pivot Rule: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows.
